title: Functions, Callbacks, Context, and Memory Interfaces · Object-Lifetime Lens: Callback Context Lifetime
summary: A plugin registry invokes callbacks after their contexts change ownership and one implementation uses an incompatible signature. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem callback context lifetime increment in typed callback and context registry. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Bind callback, context pointer, ownership, thread, and unregister lifetime into one interface contract.
Declare and call function pointers with exactly compatible prototypes and return conventions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Callback Context Lifetime · Theory Callback Context Lifetime
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory native-systems team handles memory case 4882. Produce bounded C evidence to bind callback results to explicit context lifetime; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Bind callback, context pointer, ownership, thread, and unregister lifetime into one interface contract.
